title: Score32 separated compute recost
hypothesis: Replacing the replicated score32 wrapper subtotal with separated measured dense-int8, shared score32/vector-softmax, command-dispatch, and HBM replay-controller components will expose a materially smaller score32 compute/control cost while still leaving full composed RTL integration as the gating follow-on step.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does measured-component separated compute recost make the current quality-aware score32 frontier compelling enough to prioritize composed RTL work?
include: mixed-int8 dense compute latency and traffic terms
measured score32 command-control and shared vector-softmax overhead

expected_benefit: removes the full-wrapper replication assumption from the current score32 compute/control subtotal
shows whether separated measured components materially improve score32 energy and logic area before composing RTL
gives a cleaner priority signal for the next producer-consumer composition task
risks: the recost still inherits mixed-int8 latency and external HBM/NoC/SRAM traffic unchanged

the inherited energy row uses a q8/k8/v6 reciprocal-LUT profile while the target is the q8/k8/v8 score32 exp-LUT/div profile, so quality cannot be attributed until precision-aligned RTL and schedule evidence exist
measured components are not yet embodied in one composed score32 producer-consumer RTL block
HBM energy remains source-backed aggregate energy rather than vendor current signoff

required_evaluations.objective: Recost the current quality-aware score32 Llama7B frontier using separated measured compute/control components.

expected_result.reason: The result should say whether the measured-component score32 subtotal is strong enough to justify building and measuring the composed producer-consumer RTL path next.

remaining_abstractions_after_this_step: Producer-to-score32 ready/valid queues and backpressure are not yet measured in one composed RTL block.
The inherited dense-int8 schedule has not yet been replayed against the separated score32 consumer.
Full QK-to-softmax-to-V RTL/perf-sim tensor-hash equivalence is still pending.
The inherited energy source and desired score32 target are not precision-aligned.
NoC and SRAM energy remain profile-scaled rather than gate-level toggle power.
HBM energy remains source-backed aggregate energy rather than vendor current signoff.
